name: Hamilton
description: Marion County's county seat and largest city, Hamilton anchors the county with civic landmarks, local shops, historic homes, and access to the Buttahatchee River.

name: Winfield
description: Known for Mule Day, the Pastime Theatre, and a strong downtown identity, Winfield blends small-city energy with deep local tradition.

name: Guin
description: A south Marion County city with railroad roots, MayFest, and the historic character of a community shaped by trade, timber, schools, and local gathering places.

name: Hackleburg
description: Set in northern Marion County near Dismals Canyon, Hackleburg carries stories of Jackson's Military Road, country music heritage, and resilient rebuilding.

name: Brilliant
description: Brilliant grew from the coal and railroad history of the Brilliant and Boston area, and its depot remains one of Marion County's important historic landmarks.

name: Bear Creek
description: A northern Marion County town close to Bear Creek and the Alabama-Tennessee watershed, Bear Creek is tied to schools, churches, and rural community life.

name: Gu-Win
description: Located between Guin and Winfield, Gu-Win has one of the county's most memorable names and a story connected to the area's drive-in theater history.

name: Twin
description: Also known historically as Yampertown, Twin sits near Guin, Brilliant, and Winfield and became Marion County's newest incorporated town in the early 2000s.

name: Glen Allen
description: Partly in Marion County and partly in Fayette County, Glen Allen is a small town with close ties to the county's southern communities.

name: Haleyville
description: Mostly in Winston County with a portion in Marion County, Haleyville is known regionally for its crossroads history and as the home of the first 9-1-1 call.

name: Pikeville
description: Marion County's original county seat, Pikeville remains one of the county's most important historic places and a key stop for understanding early settlement.

name: Marion County
description: Marion County's original county seat, Pikeville remains one of the county's most important historic places and a key stop for understanding early settlement.
